note: Module -> target-repository checkpoint map (spec 17-18). coursectl resets target repositories BY REVISION using this file as the source of truth; git tags are intentionally not used. Add a module by adding its key; point a module at a different target by changing its repository/revision. Modules 0-4 map to bookit at its baseline revision; module 5 maps to bookit-platform at its baseline revision; module 6 maps to bookit for its review-practice task, pending the module's lab target decision; module 7 maps to bookit-platform (the reliability lab re-runs platform tasks under injected failures); module 8 maps to bookit (the evaluation lab's task dataset targets bookit, the fast, resettable target - the eval runner makes its own fresh clones per run on top of this baseline); module 9 maps to bookit-platform (the integration lab prepares a draft pull request for a bookit-platform change; committed integration runs execute offline against a local double, so no live target is touched on setup).
